<path>/tmp/nvp_opencode_o8f7dmt2/spec.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Launch Interceptor Program Specification
2: 
3: ## Functional Requirements
4: 
5: All communication with software which calls your procedure is to be accomplished through the global variables and constant defined in this section.
6: 
7: ### Constant
8: 
9: The value of the global constant `PI` is available to your procedure, representing the number of radians in 180 degrees.
10: 
11: ### Input Variables
12: 
13: The values of the following global variables are available to your procedure:
14: 
15: | Variable                   | Description                                  |
16: | -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
17: | `X`, `Y`                  | Parallel arrays containing the coordinates of data points |
18: | `NUMPOINTS`               | The number of planar data points             |
19: | `PARAMETERS`              | Record holding parameters for LICs           |
20: | `LCM`                     | Logical Connector Matrix                     |
21: | `PUM` (diagonal elements) | Preliminary Unlocking Matrix                 |
22: 
23: ### Output Variables
24: 
25: The values of the following global variables are to be set by your procedure:
26: 
27: | Variable                       | Description                    |
28: | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------ |
29: | `PUM` (off-diagonal elements)  | Preliminary Unlocking Matrix   |
30: | `CMV`                          | Conditions Met Vector          |
31: | `FUV`                          | Final Unlocking Vector         |
32: | `LAUNCH`                       | Final launch/no launch decision |
33: 
34: ### Global Declarations
35: 
36: The global declarations have been made as follows:
37: 
38: ```pascal
39: const
40:   PI = 3.1415926535;
41: 
42: type
43:   POINTRANGE  = 1..100;
44:   LICRANGE    = 1..15;
45:   NPOINTS     = 2..100;
46:   NPTYPE      = 3..100;
47:   CONNECTORS  = (NOTUSED, ORR, ANDD);
48:   NUMQUADS    = 1..3;
49:   COORDINATE  = array[POINTRANGE] of real;
50:   CMATRIX     = array[LICRANGE, LICRANGE] of CONNECTORS;
51:   BMATRIX     = array[LICRANGE, LICRANGE] of boolean;
52:   VECTOR      = array[LICRANGE] of boolean;
53:   COMPTYPE    = (LT, EQ, GT);
54: 
55: var
56:   X          : COORDINATE;  {X coordinates of data points}
57:   Y          : COORDINATE;  {Y coordinates of data points}
58:   NUMPOINTS  : NPOINTS;     {Number of data points}
59:   PARAMETERS : record
60:     LENGTH1  : real;         {Length in LICs 1, 8, 13}
61:     RADIUS1  : real;         {Radius in LICs 2, 9, 14}
62:     EPSILON  : real;         {Deviation from PI in LICs 3, 10}
63:     AREA1    : real;         {Area in LICs 4, 11, 15}
64:     Q_PTS    : NPOINTS;      {No. of consecutive points in LIC 5}
65:     QUADS    : NUMQUADS;     {No. of quadrants in LIC 5}
66:     DIST     : real;         {Distance in LIC 7}
67:     N_PTS    : NPTYPE;       {No. of consecutive pts. in LIC 7}
68:     K_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LICs 8, 13}
69:     A_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LICs 9, 14}
70:     B_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LICs 9, 14}
71:     C_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LIC 10}
72:     D_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LIC 10}
73:     E_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LICs 11, 15}
74:     F_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LICs 11, 15}
75:     G_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LIC 12}
76:     LENGTH2  : real;         {Maximum length in LIC 13}
77:     RADIUS2  : real;         {Maximum radius in LIC 14}
78:     AREA2    : real;         {Maximum area in LIC 15}
79:   end;
80:   LCM     : CMATRIX;     {Logical Connector Matrix}
81:   PUM     : BMATRIX;     {Preliminary Unlocking Matrix}
82:   CMV     : VECTOR;      {Conditions Met Vector}
83:   FUV     : VECTOR;      {Final Unlocking Vector}
84:   LAUNCH  : boolean;     {Decision: Launch or No Launch}
85: 
86: function REALCOMPARE(A, B : real) : COMPTYPE;
87:   {compares real numbers - see Nonfunctional Requirements}
88: ```
89: 
90: ### Required Computations
91: 
92: It can be assumed that all input data and parameters that are measured in some form of units use the same, consistent units. For example, all lengths are measured in the same units that are used to define the planar space from which the input data comes. Therefore, no unit conversion is necessary.
93: 
94: Given the parameter values in the global record `PARAMETERS`, the procedure `DECIDE` must evaluate each of the Launch Interceptor Conditions (LICs) described below for the set of `NUMPOINTS` points:
95: 
96: ```
97: (X[1], Y[1]), ..., (X[NUMPOINTS], Y[NUMPOINTS])
98: ```
99: 
100: where 2 <= `NUMPOINTS` <= 100.
101: 
102: The Conditions Met Vector (CMV) should be set according to the results of these calculations, i.e., the global array element `CMV[i]` should be set to true if and only if the i-th LIC is met.
103: 
104: #### Launch Interceptor Conditions (LICs)
105: 
106: **LIC 1:** There exists at least one set of two consecutive data points that are a distance greater than the length, `LENGTH1`, apart.
107: 
108: > Constraints: (0 <= LENGTH1)
109: 
110: **LIC 2:** There exists at least one set of three consecutive data points that cannot all be contained within or on a circle of radius `RADIUS1`.
111: 
112: > Constraints: (0 <= RADIUS1)
113: 
114: **LIC 3:** There exists at least one set of three consecutive data points which form an angle such that:
115: 
116: - angle < (PI - EPSILON), or
117: - angle > (PI + EPSILON)
118: 
119: The second of the three consecutive points is always the vertex of the angle. If either the first point or the last point (or both) coincides with the vertex, the angle is undefined and the LIC is not satisfied by those three points.
120: 
121: > Constraints: (0 <= EPSILON < PI)
122: 
123: **LIC 4:** There exists at least one set of three consecutive data points that are the vertices of a triangle with area greater than `AREA1`.
124: 
125: > Constraints: (0 <= AREA1)
126: 
127: **LIC 5:** There exists at least one set of `Q_PTS` consecutive data points that lie in more than `QUADS` quadrants. Where there is ambiguity as to which quadrant contains a given point, priority of decision will be by quadrant number, i.e., I, II, III, IV. For example, the data point (0,0) is in quadrant I, the point (-1,0) is in quadrant II, the point (0,-1) is in quadrant III, the point (0,1) is in quadrant I, and the point (1,0) is in quadrant I.
128: 
129: > Constraints: (2 <= Q_PTS <= NUMPOINTS), (1 <= QUADS <= 3)
130: 
131: **LIC 6:** There exists at least one set of two consecutive data points, (X[i], Y[i]) and (X[j], Y[j]), such that X[j] - X[i] < 0 (where i = j - 1).
132: 
133: **LIC 7:** There exists at least one set of `N_PTS` consecutive data points such that at least one of the points lies a distance greater than `DIST` from the line joining the first and last of these `N_PTS` points. If the first and last points of these `N_PTS` are identical, then the calculated distance to compare with `DIST` will be the distance from the coincident point to all other points of the `N_PTS` consecutive points. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 3.
134: 
135: > Constraints: (3 <= N_PTS <= NUMPOINTS), (0 <= DIST)
136: 
137: **LIC 8:** There exists at least one set of two data points separated by exactly `K_PTS` consecutive intervening points that are a distance greater than the length, `LENGTH1`, apart. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 3.
138: 
139: > Constraints: (1 <= K_PTS <= NUMPOINTS - 2)
140: 
141: **LIC 9:** There exists at least one set of three data points separated by exactly `A_PTS` and `B_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that cannot be contained within or on a circle of radius `RADIUS1`. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 5.
142: 
143: > Constraints: (1 <= A_PTS), (1 <= B_PTS), A_PTS + B_PTS <= NUMPOINTS - 3
144: 
145: **LIC 10:** There exists at least one set of three data points separated by exactly `C_PTS` and `D_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that form an angle such that:
146: 
147: - angle < (PI - EPSILON), or
148: - angle > (PI + EPSILON)
149: 
150: The second point of the set of three points is always the vertex of the angle. If either the first point or the last point (or both) coincide with the vertex, the angle is undefined and the LIC is not satisfied by those three points. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 5.
151: 
152: > Constraints: (1 <= C_PTS), (1 <= D_PTS), C_PTS + D_PTS <= NUMPOINTS - 3
153: 
154: **LIC 11:** There exists at least one set of three data points separated by exactly `E_PTS` and `F_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that are the vertices of a triangle with area greater than `AREA1`. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 5.
155: 
156: > Constraints: (1 <= E_PTS), (1 <= F_PTS), E_PTS + F_PTS <= NUMPOINTS - 3
157: 
158: **LIC 12:** There exists at least one set of two data points, (X[i], Y[i]) and (X[j], Y[j]), separated by exactly `G_PTS` consecutive intervening points, such that X[j] - X[i] < 0 (where i < j). The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 3.
159: 
160: > Constraints: (1 <= G_PTS <= NUMPOINTS - 2)
161: 
162: **LIC 13:** There exists at least one set of two data points, separated by exactly `K_PTS` consecutive intervening points, which are a distance greater than the length, `LENGTH1`, apart. In addition, there exists at least one set of two data points (which can be the same or different from the two data points just mentioned), separated by exactly `K_PTS` consecutive intervening points, that are a distance less than the length, `LENGTH2`, apart. Both parts must be true for the LIC to be true. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 3.
163: 
164: > Constraints: (0 <= LENGTH2)
165: 
166: **LIC 14:** There exists at least one set of three data points, separated by exactly `A_PTS` and `B_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that cannot be contained within or on a circle of radius `RADIUS1`. In addition, there exists at least one set of three data points (which can be the same or different from the three data points just mentioned) separated by exactly `A_PTS` and `B_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that can be contained in or on a circle of radius `RADIUS2`. Both parts must be true for the LIC to be true. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 5.
167: 
168: > Constraints: (0 <= RADIUS2)
169: 
170: **LIC 15:** There exists at least one set of three data points, separated by exactly `E_PTS` and `F_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that are the vertices of a triangle with area greater than `AREA1`. In addition, there exist three data points (which can be the same or different from the three data points just mentioned) separated by exactly `E_PTS` and `F_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that are the vertices of a triangle with area less than `AREA2`. Both parts must be true for the LIC to be true. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 5.
171: 
172: > Constraints: (0 <= AREA2)
173: 
174: #### Combining LICs: The Preliminary Unlocking Matrix (PUM)
175: 
176: The Conditions Met Vector (CMV) can now be used in conjunction with the Logical Connector Matrix (LCM) to form the off-diagonal elements of the Preliminary Unlocking Matrix (PUM). The entries in the LCM represent the logical connectors to be used between pairs of LICs to determine the corresponding entry in the PUM, i.e., `LCM[i, j]` represents the Boolean operator to be applied to `CMV[i]` and `CMV[j]`. `PUM[i, j]` is set according to the result of this operation:
177: 
178: - If `LCM[i, j]` is **NOTUSED**, then `PUM[i, j]` should be set to **true**.
179: - If `LCM[i, j]` is **ANDD**, `PUM[i, j]` should be set to true only if (`CMV[i]` AND `CMV[j]`) is true.
180: - If `LCM[i, j]` is **ORR**, `PUM[i, j]` should be set to true if (`CMV[i]` OR `CMV[j]`) is true.
181: 
182: Note that the LCM is symmetric, i.e., `LCM[i, j]` = `LCM[j, i]` for all i and j.
183: 
184: **Example:**
185: 
186: Assume that the given Logical Connector Matrix is as shown below:
187: 
188: | LIC | 1       | 2       | 3       | 4       | 5       | ... | 15      |
189: | --- | ------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | --- | ------- |
190: | 1   | —       | ANDD    | ORR     | ANDD    | NOTUSED | ... | NOTUSED |
191: | 2   | ANDD    | —       | ORR     | ORR     | NOTUSED | ... | NOTUSED |
192: | 3   | ORR     | ORR     | —       | ANDD    | NOTUSED | ... | NOTUSED |
193: | 4   | ANDD    | ORR     | ANDD    | —       | NOTUSED | ... | NOTUSED |
194: | 5   | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | —       | ... | NOTUSED |
195: | ... | ...     | ...     | ...     | ...     | ...     | ... | ...     |
196: | 15  | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | ... | —       |
197: 
198: Also assume that the entries in the CMV have been computed as described, giving the following results:
199: 
200: | Condition | Value |
201: | --------- | ----- |
202: | 1         | false |
203: | 2         | true  |
204: | 3         | true  |
205: | 4         | true  |
206: | 5         | false |
207: | ...       | ...   |
208: | 15        | false |
209: 
210: The following PUM is generated:
211: 
212: | LIC | 1     | 2     | 3    | 4     | 5    | ... | 15   |
213: | --- | ----- | ----- | ---- | ----- | ---- | --- | ---- |
214: | 1   | \*    | false | true | false | true | ... | true |
215: | 2   | false | \*    | true | true  | true | ... | true |
216: | 3   | true  | true  | \*   | true  | true | ... | true |
217: | 4   | false | true  | true | \*    | true | ... | true |
218: | 5   | true  | true  | true | true  | \*   | ... | true |
219: | ... | ...   | ...   | ...  | ...   | ...  | ... | ...  |
220: | 15  | true  | true  | true | true  | true | ... | \*   |
221: 
222: Explanation of selected PUM entries:
223: 
224: 1. `PUM[1, 2]` is false because `LCM[1, 2]` is ANDD, and at least one of `CMV[1]` and `CMV[2]` is false.
225: 2. `PUM[1, 3]` is true because `LCM[1, 3]` is ORR, and at least one of `CMV[1]` and `CMV[3]` is true.
226: 3. `PUM[2, 3]` is true because `LCM[2, 3]` is ORR, and at least one of `CMV[2]` and `CMV[3]` is true.
227: 4. `PUM[3, 4]` is true because `LCM[3, 4]` is ANDD, and both `CMV[3]` and `CMV[4]` are true.
228: 5. `PUM[1, 5]` is true because `LCM[1, 5]` is NOTUSED.
229: 
230: #### Final Unlocking Vector (FUV)
231: 
232: The Final Unlocking Vector (FUV) is generated from the Preliminary Unlocking Matrix. The input diagonal elements of the PUM indicate whether the corresponding LIC is to be considered as a factor in signaling interceptor launch. `FUV[i]` should be set to true if `PUM[i, i]` is false (indicating that the associated LIC should not hold back launch) or if all elements in PUM row i are true.
233: 
234: **Example:**
235: 
236: Assume that the PUM now appears as follows:
237: 
238: | LIC | 1     | 2     | 3    | 4     | 5     | ... | 15    |
239: | --- | ----- | ----- | ---- | ----- | ----- | --- | ----- |
240: | 1   | true  | false | true | false | true  | ... | true  |
241: | 2   | false | false | true | true  | true  | ... | true  |
242: | 3   | true  | true  | true | true  | true  | ... | true  |
243: | 4   | false | true  | true | false | true  | ... | true  |
244: | 5   | true  | true  | true | true  | false | ... | true  |
245: | ... | ...   | ...   | ...  | ...   | ...   | ... | ...   |
246: | 15  | true  | true  | true | true  | true  | ... | false |
247: 
248: The FUV generated is as follows:
249: 
250: | Condition | Value |
251: | --------- | ----- |
252: | 1         | false |
253: | 2         | true  |
254: | 3         | true  |
255: | 4         | true  |
256: | 5         | true  |
257: | ...       | ...   |
258: | 15        | true  |
259: 
260: Explanation of selected FUV entries:
261: 
262: 1. `FUV[1]` is false because `PUM[1, 1]` is true, but `PUM[1, 2]` and `PUM[1, 4]` are false.
263: 2. `FUV[2]` is true because `PUM[2, 2]` is false.
264: 3. `FUV[3]` is true because `PUM[3, i]` is true for all i, 1 <= i <= 15.
265: 
266: #### Launch Decision
267: 
268: The final launch/no launch decision is based on the FUV. The decision to launch requires that all elements in the FUV be true, i.e., `LAUNCH` should be set to true if and only if `FUV[i]` is true for all i, 1 <= i <= 15. For the example, `LAUNCH` is false because `FUV[1]` is false.
269: 
270: ## Software Support
271: 
272: The only software facilities you may use to prepare are:
273: 
274: 1. GCC compiler
275: 
276: ## Nonfunctional Requirements
277: 
278: 1. The functional requirements are to be implemented by a parameterless Pascal procedure named `DECIDE`. It will perform no input or output, because the calling program will provide input data through global variables. Likewise, `DECIDE` should store its results in global variables.
279: 
280: 2. Whenever real numbers must be compared within the procedure `DECIDE`, that comparison should be made with a fixed amount of precision. The program which calls `DECIDE` will provide a function called `REALCOMPARE`. (See function header in declarations.) This function compares two real numbers, A and B, with respect to the six most significant digits. `REALCOMPARE` returns `LT` if A < B, `EQ` if A = B, or `GT` if A > B. `DECIDE` should call this function for all comparisons of real numbers.
281: 
282: 3. Information contained in the global variables when the subroutine is called will remain valid throughout the execution of the procedure. There are no feedback or time series effects during a call to `DECIDE`, or from multiple calls to `DECIDE`.
283: 
284: 4. Do not include input error checking. Assume that the calling program ensures inputs are complete and within the specified range.
285: 
286: 5. No double precision or complex variables should be used.
287: 
288: 6. There are no constraints on memory space or execution time, but efficient, well-structured code with descriptive comments is preferred.
289: 
290: 7. In writing the subroutine, do not use any language-dependent software tools other than the Hull V Pascal compiler.
291: 
292: ## Glossary
293: 
294: **angle**
295: : An angle is formed by two rays which share a common endpoint called a vertex. If one ray is rotated about the vertex until it coincides with the other ray, the amount of rotation required is the measure of the angle. Three points can be used to determine an angle by drawing a ray from the second point through the first point and another ray from the second point through the third point. Note that different angles are described according to whether the ray is rotated clockwise or counterclockwise. Either can be used in this problem because of the way the LICs are defined.
296: 
297: **CMV** (Conditions Met Vector)
298: : The CMV is a Boolean vector whose elements have a one-to-one correspondence with the launch interceptor conditions. If the radar tracking data satisfy a certain LIC, then the corresponding element of the CMV is to be set to true.
299: 
300: **consecutive**
301: : Two points are consecutive if they are adjacent in the input data vectors X and Y. Thus (X[i], Y[i]) and (X[i + 1], Y[i + 1]) are adjacent.
302: 
303: **diagonal element**
304: : Consider a matrix M, with n rows and n columns. The diagonal elements of the matrix are M[i, i], where i = 1, ..., n.
305: 
306: **FUV** (Final Unlocking Vector)
307: : The FUV is a Boolean vector which is the basis for deciding whether to launch an interceptor. If all elements of the FUV are true, a launch should occur.
308: 
309: **LCM** (Logical Connector Matrix)
310: : The LCM describes how individual LICs should be logically combined. For example, the value of LCM[i, j] indicates whether LIC #i should combine with LIC #j by the logical AND, OR, or not at all.
311: 
312: **LIC** (Launch Interceptor Condition)
313: : If radar tracking data exhibit a certain combination of characteristics, then an interceptor should be launched. Each characteristic is an LIC.
314: 
315: **matrix**
316: : For purposes of this problem, a matrix can be considered to be a two-dimensional array.
317: 
318: **off-diagonal element**
319: : An off-diagonal element of a matrix is any element which is not a diagonal element.
320: 
321: **planar data points**
322: : Planar data points are points that are all located within the same plane.
323: 
324: **PUM** (Preliminary Unlocking Matrix)
325: : Every element of the Boolean PUM corresponds to an element of the LCM. If the logical connection dictated by the LCM element gives the value "true," the corresponding PUM element is to be set to true.
326: 
327: **quadrant**
328: : The x and y axes of the Cartesian coordinate system divide a plane into four areas called quadrants. They are labeled I, II, III, IV, beginning with the area where both coordinates are positive and numbering counterclockwise.
329: 
330: **radius**
331: : The length of the radius of a circle is the distance from the center of the circle to any point on the circle's circumference.
332: 
333: **ray**
334: : A ray is a straight line that extends from a point.
335: 
336: **vector**
337: : For purposes of this problem, a vector may be considered to be a one-dimensional array.
338: 
339: **vertex**
340: : When two rays originate from a common point to form an angle, the point of their origination is called the vertex of that angle.

(End of file - total 340 lines)
</content>

# Launch Interceptor Program Specification

## Functional Requirements

All communication with software which calls your procedure is to be accomplished through the global variables and constant defined in this section.

### Constant

import json
import math

PI = 3.1415926535

def distance(x1, y1, x2, y2):
    return math.sqrt((x2-x1)**2 + **(y2-y1)2)

def triangle_area(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3):
    return 0.5 * abs((x2 - x1) * (y3 - y1) - (x3 - x1) * (y2 - y1))

def calc_angle(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3):
    dx1, dy1 = x1 - x2, y1 - y2
    dx2, dy2 = x3 - x2, y3 - y2
    mag1 = math.sqrt(dx1**2 + dy1**2)
    mag2 = math.sqrt(dx2**2 + dy2**2)
    if mag1 == 0 or mag2 == 0:
        return -1
    dot = dx1 * dx2 + dy1 * dy2
    ang = math.atan2(dot, mag1 * mag2)
    if ang < 0:
        ang = -ang
    return ang

def circle_radius(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3):
    a = distance(x2, y2, x3, y3)
    b = distance(x1, y1, x3, y3)
    c = distance(x1, y1, x2, y2)
    if a == 0 or b == 0 or c == 0:
        return 0
    s = (a + b + c) / 2
    area = math.sqrt(s * (s - a) * (s - b) * (s - c))
    if area == 0:
        return 0
    return (a * b * c) / (4 * area)

def quadrant(x, y):
    if x > 0 and y >= 0:
        return 1
    elif x <= 0 and y > 0:
        return 2
    elif x < 0 and y <= 0:
        return 3
    else:
        return 1

def point_to_line_distance(px, py, x1, y1, x2, y2):
    dx, dy = x2 - x1, y2 - y1
    if dx == 0 and dy == 0:
        return math.sqrt((px - x1)**2 + **(py - y1)2)
    num = abs(dy * px - dx * py + x2 * y1 - y2 * x1)
    return num / math.sqrt(dx**2 + dy**2)

import json
import math

PI = 3.1415926535

def distance(x1, y1, x2, y2):
    return math.sqrt((x2-x1)**2 + **(y2-y1)2)

def triangle_area(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3):
    return 0.5 * abs((x2 - x1) * (y3 - y1) - (x3 - x1) * (y2 - y1))

def calc_angle(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3):
    dx1, dy1 = x1 - x2, y1 - y2
    dx2, dy2 = x3 - x2, y3 - y2
    mag1 = math.sqrt(dx1**2 + dy1**2)
    mag2 = math.sqrt(dx2**2 + dy2**2)
    if mag1 == 0 or mag2 == 0:
        return -1
    dot = dx1 * dx2 + dy1 * dy2
    ang = math.atan2(dot, mag1 * mag2)
    if ang < 0:
        ang = -ang
    return ang

def circle_radius(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3):
    a = distance(x2, y2, x3, y3)
    b = distance(x1, y1, x3, y3)
    c = distance(x1, y1, x2, y2)
    if a == 0 or b == 0 or c == 0:
        return 0
    s = (a + b + c) / 2
    area = math.sqrt(s * (s - a) * (s - b) * (s - c))
    if area == 0:
        return 0
    return (a * b * c) / (4 * area)

def quadrant(x, y):
    if x > 0 and y >= 0:
        return 1
    elif x <= 0 and y > 0:
        return 2
    elif x < 0 and y <= 0:
        return 3
    else:
        return 1

def point_to_line_distance(px, py, x1, y1, x2, y2):
    dx, dy = x2 - x1, y2 - y1
    if dx == 0 and dy == 0:
        return math.sqrt((px - x1)**2 + **(py - y1)2)
    num = abs(dy * px - dx * py + x2 * y1 - y2 * x1)
    return num / math.sqrt(dx**2 + dy**2)

            angle = calc_angle(x[i], y[i], x[i+C_PTS+1], y[i+C_PTS+1], x[i+C_PTS+D_PTS+2], y[i+C_PTS+D_PTS+2])
            if angle < 0:
                continue
            if angle < (PI - EPSILON) or angle > (PI + EPSILON):
                lic10 = True
                break
    print(f"LIC10: {lic10}")
    
    # LIC 11
    lic11 = False
    if numpoints >= 5:
        for i in range(numpoints - E_PTS - F_PTS - 2):
            if triangle_area(x[i], y[i], x[i+E_PTS+1], y[i+E_PTS+1], x[i+E_PTS+F_PTS+2], y[i+E_PTS+F_PTS+2]) > AREA1:
                lic11 = True
                break
    print(f"LIC11: {lic11}")
    
    # LIC 12
    lic12 = False
    if numpoints >= 3:
        for i in range(numpoints - G_PTS - 1):
            if x[i+G_PTS+1] - x[i] < 0:
                lic12 = True
                break
    print(f"LIC12: {lic12}")
    
    # LIC 13
    lic13 = False
    if numpoints >= 3:
        has_greater = False
        has_less = False
        for i in range(numpoints - K_PTS - 1):
            d = distance(x[i], y[i], x[i+K_PTS+1], y[i+K_PTS+1])
            if d > LENGTH1:
                has_greater = True
            if d < LENGTH2:
                has_less = True
        lic13 = has_greater and has_less
    print(f"LIC13: {lic13}")
    
    # LIC 14
    lic14 = False
    if numpoints >= 5:
        has_outside = False
        has_inside = False
        for i in range(numpoints - A_PTS - B_PTS - 2):
            r = circle_radius(x[i], y[i], x[i+A_PTS+1], y[i+A_PTS+1], x[i+A_PTS+B_PTS+2], y[i+A_PTS+B_PTS+2])
            if r > RADIUS1:
                has_outside = True
            if r <= RADIUS2:
                has_inside = True
        lic14 = has_outside and has_inside
    print(f"LIC14: {lic14}")
    
    # LIC 15
    lic15 = False
    if numpoints >= 5:
        has_greater = False
        has_less = False
        for i in range(numpoints - E_PTS - F_PTS - 2):
            a = triangle_area(x[i], y[i], x[i+E_PTS+1], y[i+E_PTS+1], x[i+E_PTS+F_PTS+2], y[i+E_PTS+F_PTS+2])
            if a > AREA1:
                has_greater = True
            if a < AREA2:
                has_less = True
        lic15 = has_greater and has_less
    print(f"LIC15: {lic15}")
    
    cmv = [lic1, lic2, lic3, lic4, lic5, lic6, lic7, lic8, lic9, lic10, lic11, lic12, lic13, lic14, lic15]
    print(f"CMV: {cmv}")
    print(f"Expected CMV: {exp['cmv']}")
    print(f"CMV match: {cmv == exp['cmv']}")

<content>1: # Launch Interceptor Program Specification
2: 
3: ## Functional Requirements
4: 
5: All communication with software which calls your procedure is to be accomplished through the global variables and constant defined in this section.
6: 
7: ### Constant
8: 
9: The value of the global constant `PI` is available to your procedure, representing the number of radians in 180 degrees.
10: 
11: ### Input Variables
12: 
13: The values of the following global variables are available to your procedure:
14: 
15: | Variable                   | Description                                  |
16: | -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
17: | `X`, `Y`                  | Parallel arrays containing the coordinates of data points |
18: | `NUMPOINTS`               | The number of planar data points             |
19: | `PARAMETERS`              | Record holding parameters for LICs           |
20: | `LCM`                     | Logical Connector Matrix                     |
21: | `PUM` (diagonal elements) | Preliminary Unlocking Matrix                 |
22: 
23: ### Output Variables
24: 
25: The values of the following global variables are to be set by your procedure:
26: 
27: | Variable                       | Description                    |
28: | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------ |
29: | `PUM` (off-diagonal elements)  | Preliminary Unlocking Matrix   |
30: | `CMV`                          | Conditions Met Vector          |
31: | `FUV`                          | Final Unlocking Vector         |
32: | `LAUNCH`                       | Final launch/no launch decision |
33: 
34: ### Global Declarations
35: 
36: The global declarations have been made as follows:
37: 
38: ```pascal
39: const
40:   PI = 3.1415926535;
41: 
42: type
43:   POINTRANGE  = 1..100;
44:   LICRANGE    = 1..15;
45:   NPOINTS     = 2..100;
46:   NPTYPE      = 3..100;
47:   CONNECTORS  = (NOTUSED, ORR, ANDD);
48:   NUMQUADS    = 1..3;
49:   COORDINATE  = array[POINTRANGE] of real;
50:   CMATRIX     = array[LICRANGE, LICRANGE] of CONNECTORS;
51:   BMATRIX     = array[LICRANGE, LICRANGE] of boolean;
52:   VECTOR      = array[LICRANGE] of boolean;
53:   COMPTYPE    = (LT, EQ, GT);
54: 
55: var
56:   X          : COORDINATE;  {X coordinates of data points}
57:   Y          : COORDINATE;  {Y coordinates of data points}
58:   NUMPOINTS  : NPOINTS;     {Number of data points}
59:   PARAMETERS : record
60:     LENGTH1  : real;         {Length in LICs 1, 8, 13}
61:     RADIUS1  : real;         {Radius in LICs 2, 9, 14}
62:     EPSILON  : real;         {Deviation from PI in LICs 3, 10}
63:     AREA1    : real;         {Area in LICs 4, 11, 15}
64:     Q_PTS    : NPOINTS;      {No. of consecutive points in LIC 5}
65:     QUADS    : NUMQUADS;     {No. of quadrants in LIC 5}
66:     DIST     : real;         {Distance in LIC 7}
67:     N_PTS    : NPTYPE;       {No. of consecutive pts. in LIC 7}
68:     K_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LICs 8, 13}
69:     A_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LICs 9, 14}
70:     B_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LICs 9, 14}
71:     C_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LIC 10}
72:     D_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LIC 10}
73:     E_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LICs 11, 15}
74:     F_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LICs 11, 15}
75:     G_PTS    : POINTRANGE;   {No. of int. pts. in LIC 12}
76:     LENGTH2  : real;         {Maximum length in LIC 13}
77:     RADIUS2  : real;         {Maximum radius in LIC 14}
78:     AREA2    : real;         {Maximum area in LIC 15}
79:   end;
80:   LCM     : CMATRIX;     {Logical Connector Matrix}
81:   PUM     : BMATRIX;     {Preliminary Unlocking Matrix}
82:   CMV     : VECTOR;      {Conditions Met Vector}
83:   FUV     : VECTOR;      {Final Unlocking Vector}
84:   LAUNCH  : boolean;     {Decision: Launch or No Launch}
85: 
86: function REALCOMPARE(A, B : real) : COMPTYPE;
87:   {compares real numbers - see Nonfunctional Requirements}
88: ```
89: 
90: ### Required Computations
91: 
92: It can be assumed that all input data and parameters that are measured in some form of units use the same, consistent units. For example, all lengths are measured in the same units that are used to define the planar space from which the input data comes. Therefore, no unit conversion is necessary.
93: 
94: Given the parameter values in the global record `PARAMETERS`, the procedure `DECIDE` must evaluate each of the Launch Interceptor Conditions (LICs) described below for the set of `NUMPOINTS` points:
95: 
96: ```
97: (X[1], Y[1]), ..., (X[NUMPOINTS], Y[NUMPOINTS])
98: ```
99: 
100: where 2 <= `NUMPOINTS` <= 100.
101: 
102: The Conditions Met Vector (CMV) should be set according to the results of these calculations, i.e., the global array element `CMV[i]` should be set to true if and only if the i-th LIC is met.
103: 
104: #### Launch Interceptor Conditions (LICs)
105: 
106: **LIC 1:** There exists at least one set of two consecutive data points that are a distance greater than the length, `LENGTH1`, apart.
107: 
108: > Constraints: (0 <= LENGTH1)
109: 
110: **LIC 2:** There exists at least one set of three consecutive data points that cannot all be contained within or on a circle of radius `RADIUS1`.
111: 
112: > Constraints: (0 <= RADIUS1)
113: 
114: **LIC 3:** There exists at least one set of three consecutive data points which form an angle such that:
115: 
116: - angle < (PI - EPSILON), or
117: - angle > (PI + EPSILON)
118: 
119: The second of the three consecutive points is always the vertex of the angle. If either the first point or the last point (or both) coincides with the vertex, the angle is undefined and the LIC is not satisfied by those three points.
120: 
121: > Constraints: (0 <= EPSILON < PI)
122: 
123: **LIC 4:** There exists at least one set of three consecutive data points that are the vertices of a triangle with area greater than `AREA1`.
124: 
125: > Constraints: (0 <= AREA1)
126: 
127: **LIC 5:** There exists at least one set of `Q_PTS` consecutive data points that lie in more than `QUADS` quadrants. Where there is ambiguity as to which quadrant contains a given point, priority of decision will be by quadrant number, i.e., I, II, III, IV. For example, the data point (0,0) is in quadrant I, the point (-1,0) is in quadrant II, the point (0,-1) is in quadrant III, the point (0,1) is in quadrant I, and the point (1,0) is in quadrant I.
128: 
129: > Constraints: (2 <= Q_PTS <= NUMPOINTS), (1 <= QUADS <= 3)
130: 
131: **LIC 6:** There exists at least one set of two consecutive data points, (X[i], Y[i]) and (X[j], Y[j]), such that X[j] - X[i] < 0 (where i = j - 1).
132: 
133: **LIC 7:** There exists at least one set of `N_PTS` consecutive data points such that at least one of the points lies a distance greater than `DIST` from the line joining the first and last of these `N_PTS` points. If the first and last points of these `N_PTS` are identical, then the calculated distance to compare with `DIST` will be the distance from the coincident point to all other points of the `N_PTS` consecutive points. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 3.
134: 
135: > Constraints: (3 <= N_PTS <= NUMPOINTS), (0 <= DIST)
136: 
137: **LIC 8:** There exists at least one set of two data points separated by exactly `K_PTS` consecutive intervening points that are a distance greater than the length, `LENGTH1`, apart. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 3.
138: 
139: > Constraints: (1 <= K_PTS <= NUMPOINTS - 2)
140: 
141: **LIC 9:** There exists at least one set of three data points separated by exactly `A_PTS` and `B_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that cannot be contained within or on a circle of radius `RADIUS1`. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 5.
142: 
143: > Constraints: (1 <= A_PTS), (1 <= B_PTS), A_PTS + B_PTS <= NUMPOINTS - 3
144: 
145: **LIC 10:** There exists at least one set of three data points separated by exactly `C_PTS` and `D_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that form an angle such that:
146: 
147: - angle < (PI - EPSILON), or
148: - angle > (PI + EPSILON)
149: 
150: The second point of the set of three points is always the vertex of the angle. If either the first point or the last point (or both) coincide with the vertex, the angle is undefined and the LIC is not satisfied by those three points. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 5.
151: 
152: > Constraints: (1 <= C_PTS), (1 <= D_PTS), C_PTS + D_PTS <= NUMPOINTS - 3
153: 
154: **LIC 11:** There exists at least one set of three data points separated by exactly `E_PTS` and `F_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that are the vertices of a triangle with area greater than `AREA1`. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 5.
155: 
156: > Constraints: (1 <= E_PTS), (1 <= F_PTS), E_PTS + F_PTS <= NUMPOINTS - 3
157: 
158: **LIC 12:** There exists at least one set of two data points, (X[i], Y[i]) and (X[j], Y[j]), separated by exactly `G_PTS` consecutive intervening points, such that X[j] - X[i] < 0 (where i < j). The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 3.
159: 
160: > Constraints: (1 <= G_PTS <= NUMPOINTS - 2)
161: 
162: **LIC 13:** There exists at least one set of two data points, separated by exactly `K_PTS` consecutive intervening points, which are a distance greater than the length, `LENGTH1`, apart. In addition, there exists at least one set of two data points (which can be the same or different from the two data points just mentioned), separated by exactly `K_PTS` consecutive intervening points, that are a distance less than the length, `LENGTH2`, apart. Both parts must be true for the LIC to be true. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 3.
163: 
164: > Constraints: (0 <= LENGTH2)
165: 
166: **LIC 14:** There exists at least one set of three data points, separated by exactly `A_PTS` and `B_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that cannot be contained within or on a circle of radius `RADIUS1`. In addition, there exists at least one set of three data points (which can be the same or different from the three data points just mentioned) separated by exactly `A_PTS` and `B_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that can be contained in or on a circle of radius `RADIUS2`. Both parts must be true for the LIC to be true. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 5.
167: 
168: > Constraints: (0 <= RADIUS2)
169: 
170: **LIC 15:** There exists at least one set of three data points, separated by exactly `E_PTS` and `F_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that are the vertices of a triangle with area greater than `AREA1`. In addition, there exist three data points (which can be the same or different from the three data points just mentioned) separated by exactly `E_PTS` and `F_PTS` consecutive intervening points, respectively, that are the vertices of a triangle with area less than `AREA2`. Both parts must be true for the LIC to be true. The condition is not met when NUMPOINTS < 5.
171: 
172: > Constraints: (0 <= AREA2)
173: 
174: #### Combining LICs: The Preliminary Unlocking Matrix (PUM)
175: 
176: The Conditions Met Vector (CMV) can now be used in conjunction with the Logical Connector Matrix (LCM) to form the off-diagonal elements of the Preliminary Unlocking Matrix (PUM). The entries in the LCM represent the logical connectors to be used between pairs of LICs to determine the corresponding entry in the PUM, i.e., `LCM[i, j]` represents the Boolean operator to be applied to `CMV[i]` and `CMV[j]`. `PUM[i, j]` is set according to the result of this operation:
177: 
178: - If `LCM[i, j]` is **NOTUSED**, then `PUM[i, j]` should be set to **true**.
179: - If `LCM[i, j]` is **ANDD**, `PUM[i, j]` should be set to true only if (`CMV[i]` AND `CMV[j]`) is true.
180: - If `LCM[i, j]` is **ORR**, `PUM[i, j]` should be set to true if (`CMV[i]` OR `CMV[j]`) is true.
181: 
182: Note that the LCM is symmetric, i.e., `LCM[i, j]` = `LCM[j, i]` for all i and j.
183: 
184: **Example:**
185: 
186: Assume that the given Logical Connector Matrix is as shown below:
187: 
188: | LIC | 1       | 2       | 3       | 4       | 5       | ... | 15      |
189: | --- | ------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | --- | ------- |
190: | 1   | —       | ANDD    | ORR     | ANDD    | NOTUSED | ... | NOTUSED |
191: | 2   | ANDD    | —       | ORR     | ORR     | NOTUSED | ... | NOTUSED |
192: | 3   | ORR     | ORR     | —       | ANDD    | NOTUSED | ... | NOTUSED |
193: | 4   | ANDD    | ORR     | ANDD    | —       | NOTUSED | ... | NOTUSED |
194: | 5   | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | —       | ... | NOTUSED |
195: | ... | ...     | ...     | ...     | ...     | ...     | ... | ...     |
196: | 15  | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | NOTUSED | ... | —       |
197: 
198: Also assume that the entries in the CMV have been computed as described, giving the following results:
199: 
200: | Condition | Value |
201: | --------- | ----- |
202: | 1         | false |
203: | 2         | true  |
204: | 3         | true  |
205: | 4         | true  |
206: | 5         | false |
207: | ...       | ...   |
208: | 15        | false |
209: 
210: The following PUM is generated:
211: 
212: | LIC | 1     | 2     | 3    | 4     | 5    | ... | 15   |
213: | --- | ----- | ----- | ---- | ----- | ---- | --- | ---- |
214: | 1   | \*    | false | true | false | true | ... | true |
215: | 2   | false | \*    | true | true  | true | ... | true |
216: | 3   | true  | true  | \*   | true  | true | ... | true |
217: | 4   | false | true  | true | \*    | true | ... | true |
218: | 5   | true  | true  | true | true  | \*   | ... | true |
219: | ... | ...   | ...   | ...  | ...   | ...  | ... | ...  |
220: | 15  | true  | true  | true | true  | true | ... | \*   |
221: 
222: Explanation of selected PUM entries:
223: 
224: 1. `PUM[1, 2]` is false because `LCM[1, 2]` is ANDD, and at least one of `CMV[1]` and `CMV[2]` is false.
225: 2. `PUM[1, 3]` is true because `LCM[1, 3]` is ORR, and at least one of `CMV[1]` and `CMV[3]` is true.
226: 3. `PUM[2, 3]` is true because `LCM[2, 3]` is ORR, and at least one of `CMV[2]` and `CMV[3]` is true.
227: 4. `PUM[3, 4]` is true because `LCM[3, 4]` is ANDD, and both `CMV[3]` and `CMV[4]` are true.
228: 5. `PUM[1, 5]` is true because `LCM[1, 5]` is NOTUSED.
229: 
230: #### Final Unlocking Vector (FUV)
231: 
232: The Final Unlocking Vector (FUV) is generated from the Preliminary Unlocking Matrix. The input diagonal elements of the PUM indicate whether the corresponding LIC is to be considered as a factor in signaling interceptor launch. `FUV[i]` should be set to true if `PUM[i, i]` is false (indicating that the associated LIC should not hold back launch) or if all elements in PUM row i are true.
233: 
234: **Example:**
235: 
236: Assume that the PUM now appears as follows:
237: 
238: | LIC | 1     | 2     | 3    | 4     | 5     | ... | 15    |
239: | --- | ----- | ----- | ---- | ----- | ----- | --- | ----- |
240: | 1   | true  | false | true | false | true  | ... | true  |
241: | 2   | false | false | true | true  | true  | ... | true  |
242: | 3   | true  | true  | true | true  | true  | ... | true  |
243: | 4   | false | true  | true | false | true  | ... | true  |
244: | 5   | true  | true  | true | true  | false | ... | true  |
245: | ... | ...   | ...   | ...  | ...   | ...   | ... | ...   |
246: | 15  | true  | true  | true | true  | true  | ... | false |
247: 
248: The FUV generated is as follows:
249: 
250: | Condition | Value |
251: | --------- | ----- |
252: | 1         | false |
253: | 2         | true  |
254: | 3         | true  |
255: | 4         | true  |
256: | 5         | true  |
257: | ...       | ...   |
258: | 15        | true  |
259: 
260: Explanation of selected FUV entries:
261: 
262: 1. `FUV[1]` is false because `PUM[1, 1]` is true, but `PUM[1, 2]` and `PUM[1, 4]` are false.
263: 2. `FUV[2]` is true because `PUM[2, 2]` is false.
264: 3. `FUV[3]` is true because `PUM[3, i]` is true for all i, 1 <= i <= 15.
265: 
266: #### Launch Decision
267: 
268: The final launch/no launch decision is based on the FUV. The decision to launch requires that all elements in the FUV be true, i.e., `LAUNCH` should be set to true if and only if `FUV[i]` is true for all i, 1 <= i <= 15. For the example, `LAUNCH` is false because `FUV[1]` is false.
269: 
270: ## Software Support
271: 
272: The only software facilities you may use to prepare are:
273: 
274: 1. GCC compiler
275: 
276: ## Nonfunctional Requirements
277: 
278: 1. The functional requirements are to be implemented by a parameterless Pascal procedure named `DECIDE`. It will perform no input or output, because the calling program will provide input data through global variables. Likewise, `DECIDE` should store its results in global variables.
279: 
280: 2. Whenever real numbers must be compared within the procedure `DECIDE`, that comparison should be made with a fixed amount of precision. The program which calls `DECIDE` will provide a function called `REALCOMPARE`. (See function header in declarations.) This function compares two real numbers, A and B, with respect to the six most significant digits. `REALCOMPARE` returns `LT` if A < B, `EQ` if A = B, or `GT` if A > B. `DECIDE` should call this function for all comparisons of real numbers.
281: 
282: 3. Information contained in the global variables when the subroutine is called will remain valid throughout the execution of the procedure. There are no feedback or time series effects during a call to `DECIDE`, or from multiple calls to `DECIDE`.
283: 
284: 4. Do not include input error checking. Assume that the calling program ensures inputs are complete and within the specified range.
285: 
286: 5. No double precision or complex variables should be used.
287: 
288: 6. There are no constraints on memory space or execution time, but efficient, well-structured code with descriptive comments is preferred.
289: 
290: 7. In writing the subroutine, do not use any language-dependent software tools other than the Hull V Pascal compiler.
291: 
292: ## Glossary
293: 
294: **angle**
295: : An angle is formed by two rays which share a common endpoint called a vertex. If one ray is rotated about the vertex until it coincides with the other ray, the amount of rotation required is the measure of the angle. Three points can be used to determine an angle by drawing a ray from the second point through the first point and another ray from the second point through the third point. Note that different angles are described according to whether the ray is rotated clockwise or counterclockwise. Either can be used in this problem because of the way the LICs are defined.
296: 
297: **CMV** (Conditions Met Vector)
298: : The CMV is a Boolean vector whose elements have a one-to-one correspondence with the launch interceptor conditions. If the radar tracking data satisfy a certain LIC, then the corresponding element of the CMV is to be set to true.
299: 
300: **consecutive**
301: : Two points are consecutive if they are adjacent in the input data vectors X and Y. Thus (X[i], Y[i]) and (X[i + 1], Y[i + 1]) are adjacent.
302: 
303: **diagonal element**
304: : Consider a matrix M, with n rows and n columns. The diagonal elements of the matrix are M[i, i], where i = 1, ..., n.
305: 
306: **FUV** (Final Unlocking Vector)
307: : The FUV is a Boolean vector which is the basis for deciding whether to launch an interceptor. If all elements of the FUV are true, a launch should occur.
308: 
309: **LCM** (Logical Connector Matrix)
310: : The LCM describes how individual LICs should be logically combined. For example, the value of LCM[i, j] indicates whether LIC #i should combine with LIC #j by the logical AND, OR, or not at all.
311: 
312: **LIC** (Launch Interceptor Condition)
313: : If radar tracking data exhibit a certain combination of characteristics, then an interceptor should be launched. Each characteristic is an LIC.
314: 
315: **matrix**
316: : For purposes of this problem, a matrix can be considered to be a two-dimensional array.
317: 
318: **off-diagonal element**
319: : An off-diagonal element of a matrix is any element which is not a diagonal element.
320: 
321: **planar data points**
322: : Planar data points are points that are all located within the same plane.
323: 
324: **PUM** (Preliminary Unlocking Matrix)
325: : Every element of the Boolean PUM corresponds to an element of the LCM. If the logical connection dictated by the LCM element gives the value "true," the corresponding PUM element is to be set to true.
326: 
327: **quadrant**
328: : The x and y axes of the Cartesian coordinate system divide a plane into four areas called quadrants. They are labeled I, II, III, IV, beginning with the area where both coordinates are positive and numbering counterclockwise.
329: 
330: **radius**
331: : The length of the radius of a circle is the distance from the center of the circle to any point on the circle's circumference.
332: 
333: **ray**
334: : A ray is a straight line that extends from a point.
335: 
336: **vector**
337: : For purposes of this problem, a vector may be considered to be a one-dimensional array.
338: 
339: **vertex**
340: : When two rays originate from a common point to form an angle, the point of their origination is called the vertex of that angle.

(End of file - total 340 lines)

{"type":"step_finish","timestamp":1779807589103,"sessionID":"ses_19b338c69ffeaaZNJ7eI8r0F6A","part":{"id":"prt_e64cc92ea001TIMAOxp4KLoVig","reason":"tool-calls","messageID":"msg_e64cc81dc001TVKfsySUfM92xE","sessionID":"ses_19b338c69ffeaaZNJ7eI8r0F6A","type":"step-finish","tokens":{"total":38776,"input":38617,"output":159,"reasoning":1,"cache":{"write":0,"read":0}},"cost":0.002551705}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779807613794,"sessionID":"ses_19b338c69ffeaaZNJ7eI8r0F6A","part":{"id":"prt_e64cc9a53001Ast7VGrMP9jujB","messageID":"msg_e64cc92f20014VgrxrDDqQ9wM4","sessionID":"ses_19b338c69ffeaaZNJ7eI8r0F6A","type":"tool","tool":"write","callID":"call_f0ccf2d7980e40a88eae57be","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/tmp/nvp_opencode_o8f7dmt2/lipdecide.pas","content":"program LaunchInterceptor;

uses
  SysUtils, JSON;

const
  PI = 3.1415926535;

type
  COMPTYPE = (LT, EQ, GT);
  CONNECTORS = (NOTUSED, ORR, ANDD);
  COORDINATE = array[1..100] of real;
  CMATRIX = array[1..15, 1..15] of CONNECTORS;
  BMATRIX = array[1..15, 1..15] of boolean;
  VECTOR = array[1..15] of boolean;

var
  X, Y: COORDINATE;
  NUMPOINTS: integer;
  LENGTH1, RADIUS1, EPSILON, AREA1, DIST: real;
  Q_PTS, QUADS: integer;
  N_PTS, K_PTS, A_PTS, B_PTS, C_PTS, D_PTS, E_PTS, F_PTS, G_PTS: integer;
  LENGTH2, RADIUS2, AREA2: real;
  LCM: CMATRIX;
  PUM: BMATRIX;
  CMV: VECTOR;
  FUV: VECTOR;
  LAUNCH: boolean;

function REALCOMPARE(A, B: real): COMPTYPE;
var
  scale, eps, diff: real;
begin
  scale := Abs(A);
  if Abs(B) > scale then
    scale := Abs(B);
  if scale = 0.0 then
  begin
    REALCOMPARE := EQ;
    Exit;
  end;

  eps := 0.5e-5 * scale;
  diff := A - B;
  if diff > eps then
    REALCOMPARE := GT
  else if diff < -eps then
    REALCOMPARE := LT
  else
    REALCOMPARE := EQ;
end;

function Distance(i, j: integer): real;
begin
  Distance := Sqrt(Sqr(X[i] - X[j]) + Sqr(Y[i] - Y[j]));
end;

function TriangleArea(i, j, k: integer): real;
begin
  TriangleArea := 0.5 * Abs((X[j] - X[i]) * (Y[k] - Y[i]) - (X[k] - X[i]) * (Y[j] - Y[i]));
end;

function Angle(i, j, k: integer): real;
var
  dx1, dy1, dx2, dy2, mag1, mag2, dot: real;
begin
  dx1 := X[i] - X[j];
  dy1 := Y[i] - Y[j];
  dx2 := X[k] - X[j];
  dy2 := Y[k] - Y[j];
  mag1 := Sqrt(Sqr(dx1) + Sqr(dy1));
  mag2 := Sqrt(Sqr(dx2) + Sqr(dy2));
  if (mag1 = 0) or (mag2 = 0) then
  begin
    Angle := -1;
    Exit;
  end;
  dot := dx1 * dx2 + dy1 * dy2;
  Angle := ArcTan2(dot, mag1 * mag2);
  if Angle < 0 then
    Angle := -Angle;
end;

function CircleRadius(i, j, k: integer): real;
var
  a, b, c, s, area: real;
begin
  a := Distance(j, k);
  b := Distance(i, k);
  c := Distance(i, j);
  if (a = 0) or (b = 0) or (c = 0) then
  begin
    CircleRadius := 0;
    Exit;
  end;
  s := (a + b + c) / 2;
  area := Sqrt(s * (s - a) * (s - b) * (s - c));
  if area = 0 then
  begin
    CircleRadius := 0;
    Exit;
  end;
  CircleRadius := (a * b * c) / (4 * area);
end;

function Quadrant(x, y: real): integer;
begin
  if (x > 0) and (y >= 0) then
    Quadrant := 1
  else if (x <= 0) and (y > 0) then
    Quadrant := 2
  else if (x < 0) and (y <= 0) then
    Quadrant := 3
  else
    Quadrant := 1;
end;

function EvaluateLIC1: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC1 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - 1 do
  begin
    if Distance(i, i + 1) > LENGTH1 then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC1 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC2: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC2 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - 2 do
  begin
    if CircleRadius(i, i + 1, i + 2) > RADIUS1 then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC2 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC3: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
  angle: real;
begin
  EvaluateLIC3 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - 2 do
  begin
    angle := Angle(i, i + 1, i + 2);
    if angle < 0 then
      continue;
    if (angle < (PI - EPSILON)) or (angle > (PI + EPSILON)) then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC3 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC4: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC4 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - 2 do
  begin
    if TriangleArea(i, i + 1, i + 2) > AREA1 then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC4 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC5: boolean;
var
  i, quad_count, q1, q2, q3, q4: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC5 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - Q_PTS + 1 do
  begin
    q1 := 0; q2 := 0; q3 := 0; q4 := 0;
    quad_count := 0;
    if Quadrant(X[i], Y[i]) = 1 then q1 := 1 else if Quadrant(X[i], Y[i]) = 2 then q2 := 1 else if Quadrant(X[i], Y[i]) = 3 then q3 := 1 else q4 := 1;
    for j := 1 to Q_PTS - 1 do
    begin
      case Quadrant(X[i + j], Y[i + j]) of
        1: q1 := 1;
        2: q2 := 1;
        3: q3 := 1;
        4: q4 := 1;
      end;
    end;
    quad_count := q1 + q2 + q3 + q4;
    if quad_count > QUADS then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC5 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC6: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC6 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - 1 do
  begin
    if X[i + 1] - X[i] < 0 then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC6 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function PointToLineDistance(px, py, x1, y1, x2, y2: real): real;
var
  dx, dy, num: real;
begin
  dx := x2 - x1;
  dy := y2 - y1;
  if (dx = 0) and (dy = 0) then
  begin
    PointToLineDistance := Sqrt(Sqr(px - x1) + Sqr(py - y1));
    Exit;
  end;
  num := Abs(dy * px - dx * py + x2 * y1 - y2 * x1);
  PointToLineDistance := num / Sqrt(Sqr(dx) + Sqr(dy));
end;

function EvaluateLIC7: boolean;
var
  i, j: integer;

function REALCOMPARE(A, B: real): COMPTYPE;
var
  scale, eps, diff: real;
begin
  scale := Abs(A);
  if Abs(B) > scale then
    scale := Abs(B);
  if scale = 0.0 then
  begin
    REALCOMPARE := EQ;
    Exit;
  end;

  eps := 0.5e-5 * scale;
  diff := A - B;
  if diff > eps then
    REALCOMPARE := GT
  else if diff < -eps then
    REALCOMPARE := LT
  else
    REALCOMPARE := EQ;
end;

function Distance(i, j: integer): real;
begin
  Distance := Sqrt(Sqr(X[i] - X[j]) + Sqr(Y[i] - Y[j]));
end;

function TriangleArea(i, j, k: integer): real;
begin
  TriangleArea := 0.5 * Abs((X[j] - X[i]) * (Y[k] - Y[i]) - (X[k] - X[i]) * (Y[j] - Y[i]));
end;

function Angle(i, j, k: integer): real;
var
  dx1, dy1, dx2, dy2, mag1, mag2, dot: real;
begin
  dx1 := X[i] - X[j];
  dy1 := Y[i] - Y[j];
  dx2 := X[k] - X[j];
  dy2 := Y[k] - Y[j];
  mag1 := Sqrt(Sqr(dx1) + Sqr(dy1));
  mag2 := Sqrt(Sqr(dx2) + Sqr(dy2));
  if (mag1 = 0) or (mag2 = 0) then
  begin
    Angle := -1;
    Exit;
  end;
  dot := dx1 * dx2 + dy1 * dy2;
  Angle := ArcTan2(dot, mag1 * mag2);
  if Angle < 0 then
    Angle := -Angle;
end;

function CircleRadius(i, j, k: integer): real;
var
  a, b, c, s, area: real;
begin
  a := Distance(j, k);
  b := Distance(i, k);
  c := Distance(i, j);
  if (a = 0) or (b = 0) or (c = 0) then
  begin
    CircleRadius := 0;
    Exit;
  end;
  s := (a + b + c) / 2;
  area := Sqrt(s * (s - a) * (s - b) * (s - c));
  if area = 0 then
  begin
    CircleRadius := 0;
    Exit;
  end;
  CircleRadius := (a * b * c) / (4 * area);
end;

function Quadrant(x, y: real): integer;
begin
  if (x > 0) and (y >= 0) then

function PointToLineDistance(px, py, x1, y1, x2, y2: real): real;
var
  dx, dy, num: real;
begin
  dx := x2 - x1;

  if (dx = 0) and (dy = 0) then

  num := Abs(dy * px - dx * py + x2 * y1 - y2 * x1);

end;

  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - C_PTS - D_PTS - 2 do

    angle := Angle(i, i + C_PTS + 1, i + C_PTS + D_PTS + 2);
    if angle < 0 then
      continue;
    if (angle < (PI - EPSILON)) or (angle > (PI + EPSILON)) then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC10 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC11: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC11 := false;
  if NUMPOINTS < 5 then

  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - E_PTS - F_PTS - 2 do
  begin
    if TriangleArea(i, i + E_PTS + 1, i + E_PTS + F_PTS + 2) > AREA1 then

function EvaluateLIC12: boolean;

  if NUMPOINTS < 3 then

  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - G_PTS - 1 do

function EvaluateLIC13: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
  has_greater, has_less: boolean;
begin

  has_greater := false;
  has_less := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - K_PTS - 1 do

    if Distance(i, i + K_PTS + 1) > LENGTH1 then
      has_greater := true;
    if Distance(i, i + K_PTS + 1) < LENGTH2 then
      has_less := true;
  end;
  EvaluateLIC13 := has_greater and has_less;
end;

function EvaluateLIC14: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
  has_outside, has_inside: boolean;
begin

  if NUMPOINTS < 5 then

  has_outside := false;
  has_inside := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - A_PTS - B_PTS - 2 do
  begin
    if CircleRadius(i, i + A_PTS + 1, i + A_PTS + B_PTS + 2) > RADIUS1 then
      has_outside := true;
    if CircleRadius(i, i + A_PTS + 1, i + A_PTS + B_PTS + 2) <= RADIUS2 then
      has_inside := true;
  end;
  EvaluateLIC14 := has_outside and has_inside;

function EvaluateLIC15: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
  has_greater, has_less: boolean;
begin
  EvaluateLIC15 := false;
  if NUMPOINTS < 5 then

  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - E_PTS - F_PTS - 2 do

    if TriangleArea(i, i + E_PTS + 1, i + E_PTS + F_PTS + 2) > AREA1 then
      has_greater := true;
    if TriangleArea(i, i + E_PTS + 1, i + E_PTS + F_PTS + 2) < AREA2 then

procedure DECIDE;
var
  i, j: integer;
  all_true: boolean;
begin

  CMV[15] := EvaluateLIC15;

  for i := 1 to 15 do

    begin
      if i = j then

      else if LCM[i, j] = NOTUSED then

      else if LCM[i, j] = ANDD then

      else if LCM[i, j] = ORR then
        PUM[i, j] := CMV[i] or CMV[j];
    end;
  end;

  for i := 1 to 15 do
  begin
    all_true := true;
    for j := 1 to 15 do

    if (PUM[i, i] = false) or all_true then

      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

constructor TCharStream.Create(const S: string);
begin
  inherited Create;
  FData := S;
  FPos := 1;
end;

procedure TCharStream.SkipWhitespace;
begin
  while (FPos <= Length(FData)) and (FData[FPos] in [' ', #9, #10, #13]) do
    Inc(FPos);
end;

function TCharStream.PeekChar: char;
begin
  SkipWhitespace;
  if FPos > Length(FData) then
    Result := #0
  else
    Result := FData[FPos];
end;

function TCharStream.ReadChar: char;
begin
  SkipWhitespace;
  if FPos > Length(FData) then
    Result := #0
  else
  begin
    Result := FData[FPos];
    Inc(FPos);
  end;
end;

function TCharStream.ReadNumber: real;
var
  NumStr, ExpStr: string;
  Sign: integer;
  i: integer;
  HasDot, HasExp: boolean;
begin
  SkipWhitespace;
  Sign := 1;
  if FData[FPos] = '-' then
  begin
    Sign := -1;
    Inc(FPos);
  end
  else if FData[FPos] = '+' then
    Inc(FPos);

  NumStr := '';
  HasDot := false;
  while (FPos <= Length(FData)) and ((FData[FPos] in ['0'..'9']) or (FData[FPos] = '.')) do
  begin
    if FData[FPos] = '.' then
    begin
      HasDot := true;
      Inc(FPos);
    end
    else
    begin
      NumStr := NumStr + FData[FPos];
      Inc(FPos);
    end;
  end;

  ExpStr := '';
  if (FPos <= Length(FData)) and (FData[FPos] in ['e', 'E']) then
  begin
    ExpStr := 'e';
    Inc(FPos);
    if (FPos <= Length(FData)) and (FData[FPos] in ['+', '-']) then
    begin
      ExpStr := ExpStr + FData[FPos];
      Inc(FPos);
    end;
    while (FPos <= Length(FData)) and (FData[FPos] in ['0'..'9']) do
    begin
      ExpStr := ExpStr + FData[FPos];
      Inc(FPos);
    end;
  end;

  Result := StrToFloat(NumStr) * Power(10, StrToFloat(ExpStr)) * Sign;
end;

function TCharStream.ReadInteger: integer;
var
  NumStr: string;
  Sign: integer;
begin
  SkipWhitespace;
  Sign := 1;
  if FData[FPos] = '-' then
  begin
    Sign := -1;
    Inc(FPos);
  end
  else if FData[FPos] = '+' then
    Inc(FPos);

  NumStr := '';
  while (FPos <= Length(FData)) and (FData[FPos] in ['0'..'9']) do
  begin
    NumStr := NumStr + FData[FPos];
    Inc(FPos);
  end;

  Result := StrToInt(NumStr) * Sign;
end;

function TCharStream.ReadBoolean: boolean;
var
  Word: string;
  i: integer;
begin
  SkipWhitespace;
  Word := '';
  while (FPos <= Length(FData)) and (FData[FPos] in ['a'..'z', 'A'..'Z']) do
  begin
    Word := Word + LowerCase(FData[FPos]);
    Inc(FPos);
  end;
  Result := Word = 'true';
end;

function TCharStream.ReadString: string;
var
  Ch: char;
begin
  Ch := ReadChar;
  if Ch <> '"' then
    Exit;
  Result := '';
  while (FPos <= Length(FData)) and (FData[FPos] <> '"') do
  begin
    if FData[FPos] = '\' then
    begin
      Inc(FPos);
      if FPos <= Length(FData) then
        Result := Result + FData[FPos];
    end
    else
      Result := Result + FData[FPos];
    Inc(FPos);
  end;
  if FPos <= Length(FData) then
    Inc(FPos);
end;

function TCharStream.ReadArray: string;
var
  Ch: char;
  Items: TStringList;
begin
  Items := TStringList.Create;
  try
    Ch := ReadChar;
    if Ch <> '[' then
      Exit;
    while True do
    begin
      SkipWhitespace;
      Ch := PeekChar;
      if Ch = ']' then
      begin
        ReadChar;
        Break;
      end;
      if Ch = '"' then
        Items.Add(ReadString)
      else if Ch in ['0'..'9', '-', '+'] then
        Items.Add(FloatToStr(ReadNumber))
      else if Ch = 't' or Ch = 'f' then
        Items.Add(BooleanToStr(ReadBoolean))
      else if Ch = '[' then
        Items.Add(TCharStream.Create(FData).ReadArray)
      else if Ch = '{' then
      begin
        Inc(FPos);
        Items.Add(TCharStream.Create(FData).ReadObject);
      end;
      SkipWhitespace;
      Ch := PeekChar;
      if Ch = ',' then
        ReadChar;
    end;
    Result := Items.CommaText;
  finally
    Items.Free;
  end;
end;

function TCharStream.ReadObject: string;
var
  Ch: char;
  Key, Value: string;
  Items: TStringList;
begin
  Items := TStringList.Create;
  try
    Ch := ReadChar;
    if Ch <> '{' then
      Exit;
    while True do
    begin
      SkipWhitespace;
      Ch := PeekChar;
      if Ch = '}' then
      begin
        ReadChar;
        Break;
      end;
      Key := ReadString;
      SkipWhitespace;
      Ch := ReadChar;
      if Ch = ':' then
      begin
        SkipWhitespace;
        Ch := PeekChar;
        if Ch = '"' then
          Value := ReadString
        else if Ch in ['0'..'9', '-', '+'] then
          Value := FloatToStr(ReadNumber)
        else if Ch = 't' or Ch = 'f' then
          Value := BooleanToStr(ReadBoolean)
        else if Ch = '[' then
          Value := ReadArray
        else if Ch = '{' then
        begin
          Inc(FPos);
          Value := ReadObject;
        end;
        Items.Add(Key + ':' + Value);
      end;
      SkipWhitespace;
      Ch := PeekChar;
      if Ch = ',' then
        ReadChar;
    end;
    Result := '{' + Items.CommaText + '}';
  finally
    Items.Free;
  end;
end;

function TCharStream.Eof: boolean;
begin
  SkipWhitespace;
  Result := FPos > Length(FData);
end;

procedure ParseJSONInput(const JSONStr: string);
var
  Stream: TCharStream;
  J: string;
  i, j, idx: integer;
  Key, Val: string;
  LCMStr: string;
  PUMStr: string;
  ParamStream: TCharStream;
begin
  Stream := TCharStream.Create(JSONStr);
  try
    J := Stream.ReadObject;
    ParamStream := TCharStream.Create(J);

    Key := ''; Val := '';
    while not ParamStream.Eof do
    begin
      Key := ParamStream.ReadString;
      ParamStream.SkipWhitespace;
      ParamStream.ReadChar;
      ParamStream.SkipWhitespace;
      Val := ParamStream.ReadString;
      if Key = 'numpoints' then
        NUMPOINTS := StrToInt(Val);

    ParamStream := TCharStream.Create(J);

    begin

      if Key = 'x' then
      begin
        Val := ParamStream.ReadString;
        idx := 1;
        for i := 1 to Length(Val) do
          if Val[i] = ',' then
            Inc(idx);
      end;
    end;

    ParamStream := TCharStream.Create(J);
    Key := ''; Val := '';
    while not ParamStream.Eof do
    begin
      Key := ParamStream.ReadString;
      ParamStream.SkipWhitespace;
      ParamStream.ReadChar;
      ParamStream.SkipWhitespace;
      if Key = 'x' then
      begin
        Val := ParamStream.ReadString;
        idx := 1;
        for i := 1 to Length(Val) do
        begin
          if Val[i] in ['0'..'9', '-', '.', '+'] then
          begin
            NumStr := '';
            while (i <= Length(Val)) and (Val[i] in ['0'..'9', '-', '.', '+']) do
            begin
              NumStr := NumStr + Val[i];
              Inc(i);
            end;
            X[idx] := StrToFloat(NumStr);
            Dec(i);
            Inc(idx);
          end
          else if Val[i] = ',' then
            Inc(idx);
        end;
      end
      else if Key = 'y' then
      begin
        Val := ParamStream.ReadString;
        idx := 1;
        for i := 1 to Length(Val) do
        begin
          if Val[i] in ['0'..'9', '-', '.', '+'] then
          begin
            NumStr := '';
            while (i <= Length(Val)) and (Val[i] in ['0'..'9', '-', '.', '+']) do
            begin
              NumStr := NumStr + Val[i];
              Inc(i);
            end;
            Y[idx] := StrToFloat(NumStr);
            Dec(i);
            Inc(idx);
          end
          else if Val[i] = ',' then
            Inc(idx);
        end;
      end
      else if Key = 'parameters' then
      begin
        ParamStream.SkipWhitespace;
        ParamStream.ReadChar;
        ParamStream.SkipWhitespace;
        LCMStr := ParamStream.ReadString;
        ParamStream := TCharStream.Create(LCMStr);
        Key := ''; Val := '';
        while not ParamStream.Eof do
        begin
          Key := ParamStream.ReadString;
          ParamStream.SkipWhitespace;
          ParamStream.ReadChar;
          ParamStream.SkipWhitespace;
          Val := ParamStream.ReadString;
          case Key of
            'LENGTH1': LENGTH1 := StrToFloat(Val);
            'RADIUS1': RADIUS1 := StrToFloat(Val);
            'EPSILON': EPSILON := StrToFloat(Val);
            'AREA1': AREA1 := StrToFloat(Val);
            'Q_PTS': Q_PTS := StrToInt(Val);
            'QUADS': QUADS := StrToInt(Val);
            'DIST': DIST := StrToFloat(Val);
            'N_PTS': N_PTS := StrToInt(Val);
            'K_PTS': K_PTS := StrToInt(Val);
            'A_PTS': A_PTS := StrToInt(Val);
            'B_PTS': B_PTS := StrToInt(Val);
            'C_PTS': C_PTS := StrToInt(Val);
            'D_PTS': D_PTS := StrToInt(Val);
            'E_PTS': E_PTS := StrToInt(Val);
            'F_PTS': F_PTS := StrToInt(Val);
            'G_PTS': G_PTS := StrToInt(Val);
            'LENGTH2': LENGTH2 := StrToFloat(Val);
            'RADIUS2': RADIUS2 := StrToFloat(Val);
            'AREA2': AREA2 := StrToFloat(Val);
          end;
        end;
      end;
    end;

    ParamStream := TCharStream.Create(J);
    Key := ''; Val := '';
    while not ParamStream.Eof do
    begin
      Key := ParamStream.ReadString;
      ParamStream.SkipWhitespace;
      ParamStream.ReadChar;
      ParamStream.SkipWhitespace;
      if Key = 'lcm' then
      begin
        Val := ParamStream.ReadString;
        idx := 0;
        for i := 1 to Length(Val) do
        begin
          if Val[i] = '"' then
          begin
            Inc(i);
            LCMStr := '';
            while (i <= Length(Val)) and (Val[i] <> '"') do
            begin
              LCMStr := LCMStr + Val[i];
              Inc(i);

            if LCMStr = 'NOTUSED' then

            else if LCMStr = 'ORR' then
              LCM[idx div 15 + 1, idx mod 15 + 1] := ORR
            else
              LCM[idx div 15 + 1, idx mod 15 + 1] := ANDD;
            Inc(idx);
          end;
        end;
      end
      else if Key = 'pum_diag' then
      begin
        Val := ParamStream.ReadString;
        idx := 0;
        for i := 1 to Length(Val) do
        begin
          if Val[i] = 't' then

          end
          else if Val[i] = 'f' then
          begin
            PUM[idx + 1, idx + 1] := false;
            Inc(idx);
          end;
        end;
      end;
    end;
  finally
    Stream.Free;
  end;
end;

function GenerateJSONOutput: string;

  OutStr := OutStr + '],"launch":';

const

function REALCOMPARE(A, B: real): COMPTYPE;
var
  scale, eps, diff: real;
begin
  scale := Abs(A);
  if Abs(B) > scale then
    scale := Abs(B);
  if scale = 0.0 then
  begin
    REALCOMPARE := EQ;
    Exit;
  end;

  eps := 0.5e-5 * scale;
  diff := A - B;
  if diff > eps then
    REALCOMPARE := GT
  else if diff < -eps then
    REALCOMPARE := LT
  else
    REALCOMPARE := EQ;
end;

function Distance(i, j: integer): real;
begin
  Distance := Sqrt(Sqr(X[i] - X[j]) + Sqr(Y[i] - Y[j]));
end;

function TriangleArea(i, j, k: integer): real;
begin
  TriangleArea := 0.5 * Abs((X[j] - X[i]) * (Y[k] - Y[i]) - (X[k] - X[i]) * (Y[j] - Y[i]));
end;

function Angle(i, j, k: integer): real;
var
  dx1, dy1, dx2, dy2, mag1, mag2, dot: real;
begin
  dx1 := X[i] - X[j];
  dy1 := Y[i] - Y[j];
  dx2 := X[k] - X[j];
  dy2 := Y[k] - Y[j];
  mag1 := Sqrt(Sqr(dx1) + Sqr(dy1));
  mag2 := Sqrt(Sqr(dx2) + Sqr(dy2));
  if (mag1 = 0) or (mag2 = 0) then
  begin
    Angle := -1;
    Exit;
  end;
  dot := dx1 * dx2 + dy1 * dy2;
  Angle := ArcTan2(dot, mag1 * mag2);
  if Angle < 0 then
    Angle := -Angle;
end;

function CircleRadius(i, j, k: integer): real;
var
  a, b, c, s, area: real;
begin
  a := Distance(j, k);
  b := Distance(i, k);
  c := Distance(i, j);
  if (a = 0) or (b = 0) or (c = 0) then
  begin
    CircleRadius := 0;
    Exit;
  end;
  s := (a + b + c) / 2;
  area := Sqrt(s * (s - a) * (s - b) * (s - c));
  if area = 0 then
  begin
    CircleRadius := 0;
    Exit;
  end;
  CircleRadius := (a * b * c) / (4 * area);
end;

function Quadrant(x, y: real): integer;
begin
  if (x > 0) and (y >= 0) then
    Quadrant := 1
  else if (x <= 0) and (y > 0) then
    Quadrant := 2
  else if (x < 0) and (y <= 0) then
    Quadrant := 3
  else
    Quadrant := 1;
end;

function PointToLineDistance(px, py, x1, y1, x2, y2: real): real;
var
  dx, dy, num: real;
begin
  dx := x2 - x1;
  dy := y2 - y1;
  if (dx = 0) and (dy = 0) then
  begin
    PointToLineDistance := Sqrt(Sqr(px - x1) + Sqr(py - y1));
    Exit;
  end;
  num := Abs(dy * px - dx * py + x2 * y1 - y2 * x1);
  PointToLineDistance := num / Sqrt(Sqr(dx) + Sqr(dy));
end;

function ExtractString(const S: string; var Pos: integer): string;
var
  Start: integer;
begin
  Pos := Pos + 1;
  Start := Pos;
  while (Pos <= Length(S)) and (S[Pos] <> '"') do
    Inc(Pos);
  Result := Copy(S, Start, Pos - Start);
  Inc(Pos);
end;

function ExtractNumber(const S: string; var Pos: integer): real;
var
  Start: integer;
  NumStr: string;
begin
  Start := Pos;
  while (Pos <= Length(S)) and (S[Pos] in ['0'..'9', '-', '.', '+', 'e', 'E']) do
    Inc(Pos);
  NumStr := Copy(S, Start, Pos - Start);
  Result := StrToFloat(NumStr);
end;

function ExtractInteger(const S: string; var Pos: integer): integer;
var
  Start: integer;

begin

    Inc(Pos);
  NumStr := Copy(S, Start, Pos - Start);
  Result := StrToInt(NumStr);
end;

function ExtractBoolean(const S: string; var Pos: integer): boolean;
begin
  if Copy(S, Pos, 4) = 'true' then
  begin
    Pos := Pos + 4;
    Result := true;
  end
  else

    Pos := Pos + 5;
    Result := false;
  end;
end;

uses
  SysUtils;

const
  PI = 3.1415926535;

type
  COMPTYPE = (LT, EQ, GT);
  CONNECTORS = (NOTUSED, ORR, ANDD);
  COORDINATE = array[1..100] of real;
  CMATRIX = array[1..15, 1..15] of CONNECTORS;
  BMATRIX = array[1..15, 1..15] of boolean;
  VECTOR = array[1..15] of boolean;

var
  X, Y: COORDINATE;
  NUMPOINTS: integer;
  LENGTH1, RADIUS1, EPSILON, AREA1, DIST: real;
  Q_PTS, QUADS: integer;
  N_PTS, K_PTS, A_PTS, B_PTS, C_PTS, D_PTS, E_PTS, F_PTS, G_PTS: integer;
  LENGTH2, RADIUS2, AREA2: real;
  LCM: CMATRIX;
  PUM: BMATRIX;
  CMV: VECTOR;
  FUV: VECTOR;
  LAUNCH: boolean;
  InputData: string;

function REALCOMPARE(A, B: real): COMPTYPE;
var
  scale, eps, diff: real;
begin
  scale := Abs(A);
  if Abs(B) > scale then
    scale := Abs(B);
  if scale = 0.0 then
  begin
    REALCOMPARE := EQ;
    Exit;
  end;

  eps := 0.5e-5 * scale;
  diff := A - B;
  if diff > eps then
    REALCOMPARE := GT
  else if diff < -eps then
    REALCOMPARE := LT
  else
    REALCOMPARE := EQ;
end;

function Distance(i, j: integer): real;
begin
  Distance := Sqrt(Sqr(X[i] - X[j]) + Sqr(Y[i] - Y[j]));
end;

function TriangleArea(i, j, k: integer): real;
begin
  TriangleArea := 0.5 * Abs((X[j] - X[i]) * (Y[k] - Y[i]) - (X[k] - X[i]) * (Y[j] - Y[i]));
end;

function Angle(i, j, k: integer): real;
var
  dx1, dy1, dx2, dy2, mag1, mag2, dot: real;
begin
  dx1 := X[i] - X[j];
  dy1 := Y[i] - Y[j];
  dx2 := X[k] - X[j];
  dy2 := Y[k] - Y[j];
  mag1 := Sqrt(Sqr(dx1) + Sqr(dy1));
  mag2 := Sqrt(Sqr(dx2) + Sqr(dy2));
  if (mag1 = 0) or (mag2 = 0) then
  begin
    Angle := -1;
    Exit;
  end;
  dot := dx1 * dx2 + dy1 * dy2;
  Angle := ArcTan2(dot, mag1 * mag2);
  if Angle < 0 then
    Angle := -Angle;
end;

function CircleRadius(i, j, k: integer): real;
var
  a, b, c, s, area: real;
begin
  a := Distance(j, k);
  b := Distance(i, k);
  c := Distance(i, j);
  if (a = 0) or (b = 0) or (c = 0) then
  begin
    CircleRadius := 0;
    Exit;
  end;
  s := (a + b + c) / 2;
  area := Sqrt(s * (s - a) * (s - b) * (s - c));
  if area = 0 then
  begin
    CircleRadius := 0;
    Exit;
  end;
  CircleRadius := (a * b * c) / (4 * area);
end;

function Quadrant(x, y: real): integer;
begin
  if (x > 0) and (y >= 0) then
    Quadrant := 1
  else if (x <= 0) and (y > 0) then
    Quadrant := 2
  else if (x < 0) and (y <= 0) then
    Quadrant := 3
  else
    Quadrant := 1;
end;

function EvaluateLIC1: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC1 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - 1 do
  begin
    if Distance(i, i + 1) > LENGTH1 then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC1 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC2: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC2 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - 2 do
  begin
    if CircleRadius(i, i + 1, i + 2) > RADIUS1 then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC2 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC3: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
  angle: real;
begin
  EvaluateLIC3 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - 2 do
  begin
    angle := Angle(i, i + 1, i + 2);
    if angle < 0 then
      continue;
    if (angle < (PI - EPSILON)) or (angle > (PI + EPSILON)) then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC3 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC4: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC4 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - 2 do
  begin
    if TriangleArea(i, i + 1, i + 2) > AREA1 then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC4 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC5: boolean;
var
  i, quad_count, q1, q2, q3, q4: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC5 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - Q_PTS + 1 do
  begin
    q1 := 0; q2 := 0; q3 := 0; q4 := 0;
    case Quadrant(X[i], Y[i]) of
      1: q1 := 1;
      2: q2 := 1;
      3: q3 := 1;
      4: q4 := 1;
    end;
    for j := 1 to Q_PTS - 1 do
      case Quadrant(X[i + j], Y[i + j]) of
        1: q1 := 1;
        2: q2 := 1;
        3: q3 := 1;
        4: q4 := 1;
      end;
    quad_count := q1 + q2 + q3 + q4;
    if quad_count > QUADS then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC5 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC6: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC6 := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - 1 do
  begin
    if X[i + 1] - X[i] < 0 then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC6 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function PointToLineDistance(px, py, x1, y1, x2, y2: real): real;
var
  dx, dy, num: real;
begin
  dx := x2 - x1;
  dy := y2 - y1;
  if (dx = 0) and (dy = 0) then
  begin
    PointToLineDistance := Sqrt(Sqr(px - x1) + Sqr(py - y1));
    Exit;
  end;
  num := Abs(dy * px - dx * py + x2 * y1 - y2 * x1);
  PointToLineDistance := num / Sqrt(Sqr(dx) + Sqr(dy));
end;

function EvaluateLIC7: boolean;
var
  i, j: integer;
  max_dist: real;
begin
  EvaluateLIC7 := false;
  if NUMPOINTS < 3 then
    Exit;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - N_PTS + 1 do
  begin
    max_dist := 0;
    for j := 0 to N_PTS - 1 do
      max_dist := Max(max_dist, PointToLineDistance(X[i + j], Y[i + j], X[i], Y[i], X[i + N_PTS - 1], Y[i + N_PTS - 1]));
    if max_dist > DIST then
    begin
      EvaluateLIC7 := true;
      Exit;
    end;
  end;
end;

function EvaluateLIC8: boolean;
var

begin
  EvaluateLIC8 := false;
  if NUMPOINTS < 3 then

  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - K_PTS - 1 do

function EvaluateLIC9: boolean;
var

begin
  EvaluateLIC9 := false;
  if NUMPOINTS < 5 then

  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - A_PTS - B_PTS - 2 do

function EvaluateLIC10: boolean;

  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - C_PTS - D_PTS - 2 do

    angle := Angle(i, i + C_PTS + 1, i + C_PTS + D_PTS + 2);
    if angle < 0 then

    if (angle < (PI - EPSILON)) or (angle > (PI + EPSILON)) then

      EvaluateLIC10 := true;

function EvaluateLIC11: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
begin
  EvaluateLIC11 := false;
  if NUMPOINTS < 5 then

  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - E_PTS - F_PTS - 2 do

function EvaluateLIC12: boolean;

begin

function EvaluateLIC13: boolean;
var
  i: integer;
  has_greater, has_less: boolean;
begin
  EvaluateLIC13 := false;
  if NUMPOINTS < 3 then
    Exit;
  has_greater := false;
  has_less := false;
  for i := 1 to NUMPOINTS - K_PTS - 1 do

    if Distance(i, i + K_PTS + 1) > LENGTH1 then
      has_greater := true;
    if Distance(i, i + K_PTS + 1) < LENGTH2 then
      has_less := true;
  end;
  EvaluateLIC13 := has_greater and has_less;
end;

function EvaluateLIC14: boolean;
var

    if CircleRadius(i, i + A_PTS + 1, i + A_PTS + B_PTS + 2) <= RADIUS2 then

function EvaluateLIC15: boolean;
